name: When Agents Disagree With Themselves: Behavioral Consistency as an Uncertainty Signal for LLM Agents
summary: arXiv:2602.11619v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Running the same LLM agent on identical inputs yields 2.3-4.2 distinct action sequences per 10 runs; this behavioral variance constitutes a train...
description: arXiv:2602.11619v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Running the same LLM agent on identical inputs yields 2.3-4.2 distinct action sequences per 10 runs; this behavioral variance constitutes a training-free, black-box uncertainty signal that instantiates selective classification and distribution-free calibration for agentic systems. Across 8,000 runs of four models on 200 HotpotQA questions, con...
author: Aman Mehta
